snippet.title: HUGE News! Honda CEO Shocks Everybody!
snippet.description:
Honda’s CEO, Toshihiro Mibe, is defying the future - well, the electric one at least. Honda CEO just made a bold prediction about the future of his company and revealed a new strategy that angered many EV lovers all over the world. While other automakers like Tesla and Rivian are betting big on electric, Mibe is hesitant. Have you ever wondered why all of a sudden, EVs are being forced upon us? Why are governments all over the world suddenly banning combustion engines? Get all the answers you need in these 10 action-packed minutes. 

#ev #honda #electricvehicle

snippet.title: Honda CEO on Relation With GM, China
snippet.description:
Honda Motor Co. is shelving plans to jointly develop affordable electric vehicles with General Motors Co. due to a changing business environment, says Chief Executive Officer Toshihiro Mibe. He spoke in an interview with Bloomberg's Kurumi Mori.

snippet.title: How Honda Plans to Electrify Its Future: CEO's Views, EV Partners, and New Technologies
snippet.description:
Honda CEO’s Views on Electric Vehicles, the Company’s Electric Vehicle Manufacturers and the Future of This Automaker

Honda is one of the world’s leading automakers, known for its reliable and efficient vehicles. However, in recent years, the company has faced increasing competition and pressure from the global shift to electric vehicles (EVs). How does Honda plan to adapt to this changing market and what are its views on EVs? In this article, we will explore Honda CEO Toshihiro Mibe’s opinions on EVs, the company’s electric vehicle manufacturers and partners, and its future plans for electrification.

Honda CEO’s Views on EVs

Toshihiro Mibe became Honda’s chief executive officer in April 2021, succeeding Takahiro Hachigo. Mibe has a background in research and development, having led Honda’s R&D division before becoming CEO. He is also known for his passion for motorsports and racing.
We do NOT own all the materials as well as footages used in this video. Please contact windylovely@outlook.com for copyright matters!

snippet.title: Insane revelations Mitsubishi Almost Bought Out Honda in the 90s
snippet.description:
In this mind-blowing video, we uncover a little-known and jaw-dropping chapter in automotive history—the astonishing revelation that Mitsubishi came incredibly close to acquiring Honda in the 1990s. Join us as we delve into this fascinating tale of near-corporate dominance and the intricate events that unfolded behind the scenes.

Prepare to be astonished as we explore the historical context and the factors that led to Mitsubishi's audacious attempt to purchase one of Japan's automotive giants. We unravel the untold stories, secret negotiations, and strategic maneuvers that brought Mitsubishi and Honda to the brink of a groundbreaking deal.

Discover the motivations behind Mitsubishi's bold move, as we examine the potential synergies, market strategies, and technological advancements that fueled their desire to acquire Honda. Explore the potential impact this merger could have had on the automotive landscape and the ramifications for both companies.

Through interviews with industry insiders, historians, and experts, we shed light on the internal dynamics, challenges, and unforeseen obstacles that ultimately derailed this historic acquisition. Learn about the clashes of corporate culture, the financial complexities, and the shifting priorities that prevented the Mitsubishi-Honda deal from becoming a reality.

Prepare to have your understanding of automotive history redefined as we unveil the Insane Revelations of Mitsubishi's near-acquisition of Honda in the 90s. Don't miss out on this extraordinary video that takes you on a thrilling journey through a pivotal moment in the industry.

Subscribe now and hit the notification bell to stay updated with our latest videos that explore hidden gems of history, revealing fascinating stories that shaped our world.

#Mitsubishi #Honda #AutomotiveHistory #MergerAcquisition #IndustryRevelations

snippet.title: Interview of Toshihiro Mibe, Honda Motor Director, President and Representative, with Indian media
snippet.description:
Interview of Toshihiro Mibe, Honda Motor Co Director, President and Representative, with the Indian media on the sidelines of the Japan Mobility Show 2023. He talked about Honda's future plans in India, electrification, two-wheelers, SUVs, and so much more. It was held on October 27, 2023, at the Honda Motor headquarters in Minato City, Tokyo. Indian media asked questions in English, and Mibe San and others replied in Japanese. The communication happened via two translators.

@Honda #toshihiromibe #electrification #hondacar #japanesecars #japanmobilityshow #tokyomotorshow #honda

snippet.title: Interview | Takuya Tsumura, President & CEO, Honda Cars India
snippet.description:
Honda Cars India (HCIL) is placing a significant bet on its upcoming SUV, the Honda Elevate, unveiled in New Delhi. With high expectations, the company aims a growth of 35% from October 2023 - March 2024.

Takuya Tsumura, President and CEO of HCIL, discusses the company's resurgence in the SUV segment with the Elevate while emphasising a continued focus on sedans alongside SUVs for their upcoming portfolio and more.

0:49 - SUV segment interest
1:26 - Growth strategy
2:00 - Upcoming EV based on Elevate
2:28 - Future of sedans
2:43 - Importance of the City and Amaze
3:23 - New SUVs to be launched
4:00 - Return of the CR-V 
4:31 - Honda Sensing

Honda CEO: "We Are Facing Billions In Losses & Forced Into Bankruptcy!"
snippet.description:
In this informative video, we look at the challenges faced by Honda Motor Co. and the techniques used by its CEO, Toshihiro Mibe, to steer the firm through challenging times. Join us as we examine Honda's financial difficulties, potential shutdown concerns, and Mibe's initiatives to restore the company's fortunes.

The worldwide economic slowdown and the pandemic have both had a substantial influence on Honda's financial stability. Honda has an uncertain future with billions of dollars in losses. Toshihiro Mibe, on the other hand, has emerged as a leader with a thorough plan to get the company back on track.

Mibe's tactics include streamlined operations, investments in innovation, and a focus on sustainability. Mibe intends to increase operational efficiency and profitability by focusing on Honda's core capabilities, optimising supply chains, and cutting expenses. In addition, he understands the value of innovation and is devoted to developing electric cars (EVs) and autonomous driving technologies in order to stay ahead of industry trends.

This video examines the possible outcomes for Honda's future, taking into account the company's strengths, weaknesses, and competitive landscape. We compare Honda's status to that of other major automakers, like as Toyota and General Motors, in order to get useful insight into the problems and opportunities that lie ahead.

Join us as we examine Toshihiro Mibe's leadership, Honda's strategic ambitions, and their implications for the company's recovery journey. Learn how Honda intends to adapt to a constantly changing automotive sector and investigate the prospects of creative collaborations. Can Honda overcome its financial difficulties and reclaim its place as a premier automaker? Watch to find out!

Don't miss this enthralling examination of Honda's present problems and CEO Toshihiro Mibe's imaginative initiatives. Subscribe to our channel if you want to learn more about the automotive sector.

snippet.title: CEO OF HONDA: We're bringing back a classic model.
snippet.description:
Welcome to Car Time! In this video, we bring you the most exciting automotive news that has taken the world by storm. Honda, the renowned Japanese automaker, has officially announced the revival of one of its most iconic vehicles, the Honda S2000! Enthusiasts and fans of this beloved sports car are overjoyed with this long-awaited news.

Reviving classic nameplates has become a profitable trend for car manufacturers, as we've seen with the success of Ford's revamped Maverick and Acura's revival of the NSX. Now, Honda is ready to make a splash in the automotive world with the return of the S2000, a car that gained legendary status thanks to its appearance in "Gran Turismo" and "2 Fast 2 Furious."

The original Honda S2000 was celebrated for its exceptional performance and driving features, making it one of the top sports cars of its time. Its high-revving 2.0-liter engine, paired with a precise six-speed manual gearbox, delivered a thrilling and engaging driving experience, delighting enthusiasts around the world.



#HondaS2000 #RevivedIcon #NextGenS2000 #SportsCarRevival #AutomotiveNews #CarEnthusiasts #IconicRoadster #SleekDesign #HighPerformance #DrivingExperience #ConceptCar #HondaGMPartnership #HybridSportsCar #ElectricVehicleTechnology #TurbochargedPower #ExcitingReveal #CarTime

snippet.title: Honda CEO Had Enough & Just SHOCKED The Entire Car Industry!
snippet.description:
We examine Honda's historic choice to forgo traditional dealerships in favour of a direct-to-consumer business model in today's in-depth analysis. Honda is a pioneer in the automotive business, so its paradigm change is sure to cause a stir and possibly change the way we buy automobiles in the future. We'll talk about the elements causing this shift, such as the emergence of autonomous technology and electric cars as well as the general digitization of the customer experience.

Often regarded as the auto industry maverick, Tesla has already been operating without dealerships, providing an early look into what the future of automobile shopping may hold. But now that the industry mainstay Honda is involved, we have to ask if other behemoths like Toyota, Nissan, and Suzuki will follow suit. These well-known names are not the only ones undergoing change. All of the luxury automakers, including Ferrari, Bentley, and Rolls Royce, may want to reevaluate their approaches to selling their vehicles.

Changes in sales locations are not the only thing at play. The effects extend to the environment, urban real estate, and employment markets. Younger consumers' changing shopping habits, which emphasise the transparency and immediacy of online shopping, are also crucial to this shift.

Come along as we examine this seismic change, evaluate Honda's approach against rivals, and project how automobile purchases will develop in the future. This video promises insights and analyses that you won't want to miss, whether you're a Tesla enthusiast, a devoted Honda fan, or you're just interested in learning more about the future of the automobile industry. Don't forget to subscribe, like, and share for more in-depth conversations about the most recent business trends!

Honda is a pioneer in the automotive business, so its paradigm change is sure to cause a stir and possibly change the way we buy automobiles in the future. We'll talk about the elements causing this shift, such as the emergence of autonomous technology and electric cars as well as the general digitization of the customer experience.

Often regarded as the auto industry maverick, Tesla has already been operating without dealerships, providing an early look into what the future of automobile shopping may hold. But now that the industry mainstay Honda is involved, we have to ask if other behemoths like Toyota, Nissan, and Suzuki will follow suit. These well-known names are not the only ones undergoing change. All of the luxury automakers, including Ferrari, Bentley, and Rolls Royce, may want to reevaluate their approaches to selling their vehicles.

Changes in sales locations are not the only thing at play. The effects extend to the environment, urban real estate, and employment markets. Younger consumers' changing shopping habits, which emphasise the transparency and immediacy of online shopping, are also crucial to this shift.

Come along as we examine this seismic change, evaluate Honda's approach against rivals, and project how automobile purchases will develop in the future. This video promises insights and analyses that you won't want to miss, whether you're a Tesla enthusiast, a devoted Honda fan, or you're just interested in learning more about the future of the automobile industry. Don't forget to subscribe, like, and share for more in-depth conversations about the most recent business trends!

snippet.title: Kenneth Shinozuka - Three hierarchical levels of analysis on psychedelics
snippet.description:
Three hierarchical levels of analysis on psychedelics: a comprehensive meta-analysis

Serotonergic psychedelics induce altered states of consciousness and have shown potential in treating a variety of neuropsychiatric disorders including depression and addiction. Yet, their modes of action are currently not fully understood. Here we provide a systematic review of the tripartite hierarchy of levels underlying the effects of psychedelics, from their molecular pharmacology to the associated whole-brain activity and subjective experience (phenomenology). As part of this, we report the first comprehensive, systematic meta-analysis of functional neuroimaging studies on psychedelics based on the published results. Our systematic meta-analysis links each of the classic psychedelics (psilocybin, LSD and ayahuasca) to specific fingerprints at each level of this tripartite hierarchy. The results show a highly non-linear relationship between these fingerprints from pharmacology, neuroimaging, and phenomenology. Additionally, the neuroimaging analysis reveals surprising findings such as increased connectivity within the default mode network on psychedelics, apparently contradicting a major theme of the literature. Overall, the results point to the need for standardising reporting tools and for more research on the emergence between different levels of psychedelic effects. Future work could use our tripartite hierarchy framework to investigate the dose -dependent effects of different psychedelics in the same participants.

snippet.title: Interview | Kunal Behl, VP, Marketing and Sales, Honda Cars India
snippet.description:
Japanese carmaker's Indian arm Honda Cars India (HCIL) is betting big on its upcoming SUV - Honda Elevate - which was recently unveiled in New Delhi. The company pins strong hopes from the SUV, which is expected to retain loyalists, as well as bring new customers into the Honda family. 

Kunal Behl, VP, Marketing & Sales, HCIL, talks about the Elevate becoming the third pillar of growth for Honda, and bringing incremental volumes of up to 35 percent in the H2 FY24, with its slated launch during the festive season of this year.

#elevate #honda #suv #newsuv #adas #ivtec

snippet.title: Livestream of Press Briefing regarding TMC's New Organization Policy
snippet.description:
Toyota Motor Corporation (TMC) will hold a press briefing regarding our new organization policy in Tokyo on April 7, 2023 from 13:30 JST.

The press briefing will be live streamed (with English interpretation) on this webpage. Video from the presentation will be available on-demand after the live stream.

In addition to this site, the live stream can be watched on TMC's official YouTube channel. A reminder can be set by logging in to your YouTube account and accessing the above YouTube Live URL.

Date / time
Friday, April 7, 2023 13:30-15:00 JST

Language
Japanese with English interpretation

Speakers
Koji Sato, President
Hiroki Nakajima, Executive Vice President
Yoichi Miyazaki, Executive Vice President, and others

Contents
Presentation, Q&A session, etc.



_____________________
_____________________

Toyota Motor Corporation Official Global Website: https://global.toyota/en/
Toyota Motor Corporation on Twitter: http://www.twitter.com/ToyotaMotorCorp/

snippet.title: Getting to Know the Akita: A Breed of Loyalty, Intelligence, and Beauty
snippet.description:
Are there any famous Akita owners?
The Akita is a magnificent and majestic breed that has captured the hearts of many dog lovers around the world
  Known for their loyalty, intelligence, and striking appearance, Akitas have become a popular choice among dog enthusiasts
  Over the years, this breed has earned a reputation for being a favorite among the rich and famous
One of the most famous Akita owners was the renowned actor, Richard Gere
  Gere, known for his roles in films such as "Pretty Woman" and "An Officer and a Gentleman," has openly expressed his love and admiration for Akitas
  In fact, he owned an Akita named Hachiko, who he adopted while filming the movie "Hachi: A Dog's Tale
 " Hachiko's loyalty and devotion are the inspiration behind the film and have undoubtedly played a role in showcasing the Akita breed to a wider audience
Another well-known Akita owner is the world-renowned veterinarian, Dr
  Marty Becker
  Dr
  Becker is recognized for his television appearances on popular shows such as "Good Morning America" and "The Dr
  Oz Show
 " His passion for animals led him to adopt an Akita named Quixote, who quickly became a beloved member of his family
  Dr
  Becker often shares his experiences with Quixote on social media, highlighting the breed's intelligence and unique characteristics
Japanese actor and fashion model, Nissy, is also a proud owner of an Akita
  Nissy, whose real name is Takahiro Nishijima, gained fame as a member of the popular J-pop band, "EXILE
 " He has expressed his love for Akitas in various interviews and social media posts
